title: Melted Mediterranean Polish Cheese Casserole
description: A cozy Polish dish featuring onions, cured meat, and Mediterranean-friendly flavors, served in a scallop shell, with a jammy and cheese-pull texture.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, saute the sliced onions over medium heat until they are caramelized and golden brown.
Add the cured sausage to the skillet and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
In a large bowl, combine the feta cheese, ricotta cheese, kalamata olives, sun-dried tomatoes, and cooked onion and sausage mixture.
Stir until all the ingredients are well combined.
Spoon the cheese mixture into the scallop shells, filling them to the top.
Place the filled scallop shells on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the casserole in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove the casserole from the oven and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley.
Serve the casserole hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
